svg.text: PHYSICAL DATA PATHS: GPU ↔ CPU MEMORY AND GPU ↔ NVMe STORAGE
Physical Topologies and Data Flow Paths in Modern Servers
(GPU to Host Memory and Host Memory to GPU)
• The GPU's DMA engine initiates PCIe transactions
• Transactions traverse the PCIe fabric (may include
switches) to reach the CPU Root Complex.
• The CPU Root Complex routes the requests to the
• The memory controller drives DDR commands to the
• CPU cores are NOT in the data path — they only
set up descriptors, not copy the payload.
• Bandwidth is bounded by the PCIe link (Gen3/4/5)
and the DRAM subsystem (channels × DDR rate).
• Pinned / page-locked host buffers avoid OS paging
and enable zero-copy DMA from the kernel side.
• No CPU memcpy on the data path.
• Every request must go through the CPU Root
Complex; DRAM is only reachable via the memory
• Bandwidth ceiling = min(PCIe link, DRAM BW).
• Latency includes PCIe traversal + Root Complex
• On multi-socket systems, crossing UPI / Infinity
this path entirely (out of scope here).
• Bounce-buffer overhead only appears for storage
PHYSICAL PATH (With CPU Memory Bounce Buffer)
• NVMe controller DMA reads data from NAND and writes to system DRAM.
• GPU DMA reads data from system DRAM and writes to its VRAM.
• Two PCIe data transfers + CPU memory bounce buffer.
• Buffered I/O: 2 memcpy per transfer; O_DIRECT: 1 memcpy.
• Applies symmetrically to Read (NVMe→GPU) and Write (GPU→NVMe).
PHYSICAL PATH (Direct Storage to GPU Memory)
Direct Data Path (No CPU Memory Involved)
• NVMe DMA writes directly into GPU VRAM; CPU memory entirely bypassed.
• Stack (NV): libcufile API + nvidia-fs.ko | AMD analog: DirectGMA + ROCm / DMA-BUF + upstream pci_p2pdma.
• Legacy mode: nvidia-fs.ko + MOFED-patched NVMe driver (pre-CUDA 12.8).
• Modern mode: Linux ≥ 6.2 upstream pci_p2pdma + CUDA 12.8+ (no MOFED patch).
• GPU sits on Socket 0. Accessing Socket 1 local DRAM
traverses UPI / Infinity Fabric — extra latency and
• Some platforms disable cross-socket P2P entirely.
• NUMA affinity: pin workload to same socket as GPU.
• Same-switch P2P has lowest latency — recommended
GDS layout (PIX label in nvidia-smi topo -m).
• When NVMe and GPU share the same Root Complex
(no switch), GDS still works, subject to Root Complex
• Functional, but not the most efficient topology.
• Typically shows as PHB/PXB in nvidia-smi topo -m.
• Prefer a dedicated PCIe Switch (3-B) for production
• Some server CPUs disable RC-internal P2P altogether.
1. PCIe is the underlying physical transport in all
2. GPU cannot reach system DRAM without going
through the CPU Root Complex + Memory Ctrl.
3. GDS allows storage DMA to hit GPU VRAM directly,
4. Best GDS topology: same PCIe Switch (PIX). Same-
RC (PHB/PXB) is functional but less optimal.
5. NVLink GPU↔GPU paths are out of scope here.
6. GPU↔GPU peer paths classified in Section 4.
GPU↔GPU peer paths within a single host. Left → Right = Best → Worst.
Latency: very low | BW: full PCIe
Left → Right: latency increases ↑ · effective bandwidth decreases ↓
P2P Badge Key: ✓ supported (PIX, PXB) · △ platform-dependent (PHB, NODE — via Host Bridge, kernel allowlist) · ✗ not supported (SYS — cross-socket)
GPUDirect P2P ⊆ PCIe P2PDMA capability; NVLink (NV#) is an additional non-PCIe path, out of scope here.
GPU ↔ CPU 内存 / GPU ↔ NVMe 存储 / GPU↔GPU 对等拓扑分级 — 整合注释版
